title: Chapter 2 - Setting Up Git and VS Code
description: Check your understanding of Git setup and VS Code Git features.

questions.prompt: What does `git config --global user.name` mainly affect?

options.label: The name shown as the author in your commits
The name of your main branch

explanations.overview: Git config sets commit metadata like author name and email.

byOptionId.a: Correct. It sets the author name for commits.
byOptionId.b: Not quite. GitHub usernames are separate from Git config.
byOptionId.c: Not quite. This does not rename your computer.
byOptionId.d: Not quite. Branch names are not set by `user.name`.

questions.prompt: Which VS Code feature helps you stage changes and write commit messages?

explanations.overview: VS Code's Source Control view integrates with Git for staging and committing.

byOptionId.a: Not quite. Explorer shows files, but staging happens in Source Control.
byOptionId.b: Not quite. Debug is for running and debugging code.
byOptionId.c: Correct. Source Control is where you stage and commit.
byOptionId.d: Not quite. Extensions can add features, but Source Control is the main Git UI.

options.label: A folder that stores Git history and data for the repo
A folder that stores your VS Code settings
A folder that stores images for GitHub
A folder that makes your code faster

explanations.overview: The `.git` folder contains the repo database: commits, branches, and more.

byOptionId.a: Correct. `.git` is where Git keeps the repo data.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. VS Code settings are stored elsewhere.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. Images are not stored in `.git` by default.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. It does not change code performance.

questions.prompt: In most diffs, what do green and red usually mean?

options.label: Green = added lines, Red = removed lines
Green = old code, Red = new code
They have no meaning at all

explanations.overview: Diff colors commonly show added (green) and removed (red) content.

byOptionId.a: Not quite. Green is added and red is removed in typical diffs.
byOptionId.b: Correct. Green is added and red is removed in typical diffs.
byOptionId.c: Not quite. Green usually marks new additions, not old code.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. They are meaningful change indicators.
